title: Clean Fuels Weekly Commentary: Type 1 Credit Prices Record a Rise for the Third Consecutive Week; Type 1 Volumes Dip by Nearly 60%
content:
This WeekГўв‚¬в„ўs Highlights:

(7th JulyГ‚В  Гўв‚¬вЂњГ‚В  13th July 2025)
Гўв‚¬Вў A total of 151,710 credits were exchanged as Type 1 trades, a 58.05% fall week-over-week (WoW), and all non-zero trades witnessed a 114.40% rise WoW as 2,346,691 credits were traded in CA LCFS.
Гўв‚¬Вў The weekly average CA LCFS credit price stood at $44.88/MT for Type 1 transfers, recording a 0.70% rise WoW; All Non-Zero transfers rose by 27.91% in price, reaching $65.36/MT.
Гўв‚¬Вў The average LCFS spot credit price fell by 6.72% WoW; on 15th July, the spot price stood at $46.50/MT, and the benchmark price stood at $51.00/MT.
Гўв‚¬Вў D3 RIN prices witnessed a rise of 1.4% WoW. D4, D5 prices remained unchanged, while D6 RIN prices decreased by 0.92% WoW.

cCarbonГўв‚¬в„ўs Market View

In the past week, prices for Type 1 trades rose marginally, while spot prices recorded a drop after recording a consistent rise for three consecutive weeks. Industry associations welcomed the higher RVOs as supportive of American farmers, energy security, and rural economies, but want the EPA to be strict with small refinery waivers (SREs) and to ensure any lost volumes are replaced. Some groups pushed the EPA to increase conventional volumes above the 15-billion-gallon threshold, and to strengthen targets for advanced biofuels. Uncertainty remains over SRE handling and the impact of tighter import rules. The proposal marks a strong federal commitment to domestic biofuels, but the details will be shaped by continued industry and public input. cCarbon hosted its webinar Гўв‚¬Е“Post-OAL Decision: WhatГўв‚¬в„ўs Next for the California LCFS Market?Гўв‚¬Вќ on 15th July 2025, joined by Cory-Ann Wind from Clean Fuels Alliance America. The webinar provided an updated analysis of CaliforniaГўв‚¬в„ўs stricter LCFS regulation, including credit bank status, supply-demand projections, price projections and the outlook for major credit sources, using cCarbonГўв‚¬в„ўs CFS.CarbonOutlook model. Read our article to know more!

CA LCFS Weekly Price and Volume Trend

The average Type 1 prices recorded a 0.70% WoW rise, increasing from $44.57 to $44.88 this week, and all non-zero credit prices increased by 27.91% WoW, from $51.10 to $65.36. Type 1 trades saw 151,710 credits exchanged, reflecting a 58.05% fall WoW. The total transaction volumes for all non-zero trades saw a 114.40% rise WoW, reaching 2,346,691 credits. [caption id="attachment_600547" align="alignnone" width="800"] Fig 1. Weekly average credit prices and weekly volumes, Source: California ARB[/caption]

CA LCFS Daily Average Spot Price

Last week, the average LCFS spot credit prices decreased by 6.72% WoW, reaching $44.25/MT. On 15th July, the spot price was $46.50/MT. The benchmark price, representing the future price of contracts expiring at the end of 2025, stood at $51.00/MT as of 15th July. [caption id="attachment_600548" align="alignnone" width="802"] Fig 2. California LCFS Weekly average spot prices and benchmark (*CC.Info OTC: Numbers are an average of broker prices reported to CC.info)[/caption]

Г‚В CA LCFS Weekly Trade Frequency and Volume

As per the data reported by CARB last week, 94 trades were reported in the market, with 30 high-volume trades. The highest transfer by price was $81.50 for one Type 2 transaction at a volume of 50,000 credits. Last week, the lowest transfer by price was $41.00 per credit of two Type 1 transactions reported for 5,000 and 20,000 credits, respectively. The smallest volume transfers of 131 credits of the Type 2 category were undertaken for a price of $42.19, while the largest volume transfers of 300,000 credits of the Type 2 category were undertaken at $52.40. [caption id="attachment_600550" align="alignnone" width="1000"] Fig 3. Trade frequency and volume, Source: California ARB[/caption]

Г‚В CA LCFS Monthly Volumes and Price Trend

The average spot Type 1 price stood at $45.77 in July so far, up from $44.06 in June. Trade volumes for the month of July stood at 2.88M. [caption id="attachment_600551" align="alignnone" width="802"] Fig 4. Total monthly volumes and associated prices, Source: California ARB[/caption]

CA LCFS Weekly Volume Price Trend

The Volume Price Trend Indicator (VPT) helps identify the parity between supply and demand for a stock. The idea behind the indicator is to multiply the market volume of LCFS credits by the percentage change in its price. If the credit price declines, the value of the indicator falls. Across 7th JulyГ‚В Гўв‚¬вЂњ 13th July, total VPT recorded a rise 2.24x WoW, with a rise in all non-zero credit prices for the last week. [caption id="attachment_600552" align="alignnone" width="800"] Fig 5. Volume Price Trend indicator for the CA LCFS market, Source: California ARB[/caption]

Г‚В Renewable Fuel Standard (RFS): RIN Weekly Price Trend

RINs are credits used for compliance and are the Гўв‚¬Е“currencyГўв‚¬Вќ of the RFS program. Renewable fuel producers generate RINs, market participants trade RINs, and obligated parties obtain and then ultimately retire RINs for the compliance list. D3 RIN prices witnessed a rise of 1.40% WoW. D4, D5 prices remained unchanged, while D6 RIN prices decreased by 0.92% WoW. D3: Cellulosic biofuel D4: Biomass-based diesel D5: Advanced biofuels (produced from any type of renewable biomass except corn starch ethanol) D6: Conventional renewable biofuel ethanol derived from corn starch, or any other qualifying renewable fuel

Upcoming Dates

Г‚В Key Clean Fuels Market Developments this Week

  • Gevo Sells $22M of Clean Fuel Production Credits: In Colorado, on June 30, 2025, Gevo Intermediate HoldCo, LLC entered into a Tax Credit Transfer Agreement with a transferee bank pursuant to which Transferor agreed to supply Гўв‚¬Е“Clean Fuel Production CreditsГўв‚¬Вќ to Transferee from the production of ethanol between January 31, 2025 and December 31, 2025 by TransferorГўв‚¬в„ўs wholly owned subsidiary, Net-Zero Richardton, LLC. Under the Agreement, Transferor expects to deliver $22 million worth of credits to Transferee between June 30, 2025 and December 10, 2025 upon satisfaction of certain conditions precedent on each transfer date, with $5 million worth of credits being transferred at the time of execution of the Agreement.
  • Landus Creates New Premium Opportunities with Clean Fuel Regulation Initiative: Iowa farmers have a new market opportunity for their 2025 soybean crop. Landus is expanding its Clean Fuel Regulation (CFR) initiative, made possible by recent policy changes expected to increase Canada's demand for liquid biofuel. This initiative is in partnership with Verity, a digital platform built to track, trace, and validate sustainability attributes such as carbon data. As of 2025, soy oil processed at the Ralston Landus location qualifies for this premium market with chain-of-custody documentation that begins with the farmer. This opportunity has resulted in approximately $750,000 in additional premiums for participants on their 2024 crop. Landus will begin distributing these premiums to participating farmers after the end of its fiscal year.
